text:
With the Paris Olympics coming up in a few weeks,Nike's going all-out with the special edition sneaks. Among them is the appropriately-named Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics,’ and while, yes, it's a pretty solidtrainerby itself, old school Jordan Brand stans can't help but feel like they've seen it somewhere before.

Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics’

Landing in a combo of ‘Smoke Grey,’ ‘Iron Grey,’ ‘Cement Grey,’ and ‘Particle Grey’ (all the greys!), theseJordanswork on top of a suede base, and are overlaid by textured leather panels throughout.Michael Jordan's signature Jumpman logo is stamped to the tongue tag and heel badge, while mesh features on the sidewalls to keep things nice and breezy.

The rest of the shoe is built on top of a chunky midsole, and this is fitted with an Air bubble around the heel. It's then finished off with a star-spangled outsole, which is similar to the one found on the Air Force 1.

Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics’

This year marks the AJ4's 35th anniversary, so the American sportswear company's pulled out all the stops for this one. But with its all-grey colour combo and its suede-leather construction, it looks almost identical to the Kaws x Air Jordan 4 ‘Cool Grey’ that released in 2017. Made in collaboration with thelegendary artist, they sold out in literal seconds back then. So if you didn't manage to cop, this is your next best bet.

Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics’

The Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics’ is set to drop on July 27 at Nike, the Snkrs app, andselect shopsglobally for around £180 – a small price to pay, considering the Kaws collab currently resells for a few grand.

title: The Air Jordan 4 SE ‘Paris Olympics’ could easily be mistaken for a 2017 grail | British GQ
author: Adam Cheung
